description: The right side of 'or' welcomes you just the same.

hints.text: This is the mirror of the previous problem. The goal is $B \to (A \lor B)$ — if $B$ is true, then $A$ or $B$ is true. The logic is symmetric. Start by assuming $B$.

Last time you used `or-intro-l` to put your truth on the left of $\lor$. This time $B$ belongs on the **right**. Which variant lets you supply the right-hand truth and place an arbitrary [formula|formula] on the left?
Use `or-intro-r` — from $B$ at step 1, form $A \lor B$ with $A$ on the left.
